sources.notes: Statement and constraints for the hard version.
Official Round 1082 tutorial checked as the main source.
Used to cross-check the depth-prefix-minimum interpretation.

annotation.summary: For every subarray, compute the minimum number of initial roots needed to generate it under the lost-civilization preorder rule, and sum this value over all subarrays.
annotation.constraints: The hard version has large total n, so all subarrays cannot be processed separately; a linear contribution method is needed.
annotation.core_idea: Parse the sequence as preorder forest subtrees and record each position's depth. For a subarray, the answer is the number of positions that become a new prefix minimum depth inside that subarray. Each position contributes to left endpoints after the previous position with strictly smaller depth and to all right endpoints at or after it; a monotonic stack finds the previous-smaller boundary.
annotation.complexity: O(n) per test case after parsing depths.
tricks: convert generated forest to depth array
subarray answer equals count of prefix depth minima

solution_variants.name: depth-prefix-minima-contribution
solution_variants.summary: Parse the preorder forest into depths, then sum each index's contribution using the nearest previous strictly smaller depth.

tags.evidence: The final summation needs the nearest previous position with strictly smaller depth for every index.
The generated-subarray value is transformed into counting prefix minima in the depth array.
Each position is counted over all subarrays where it is a new prefix minimum.
The sequence is interpreted as a preorder forest, but no general tree algorithm is needed.
